pairs.question: What was the date and location of the 2006 Bombardier Learjet 500 IndyCar race?
pairs.answer: The 2006 Bombardier Learjet 500 was held on June 10, 2006, at Texas Motor Speedway in Fort Worth, Texas.

pairs.question: Who won the 2006 Bombardier Learjet 500 IndyCar race, and how many laps did the race consist of?
pairs.answer: Marlboro Team Penske driver Hélio Castroneves won the 200-lap race.

pairs.question: Which driver secured the pole position for the 2006 Bombardier Learjet 500, and what notable achievement did he have two weeks prior?
pairs.answer: Sam Hornish Jr. won the pole position with the fastest qualifying time. Two weeks before the race, he had won the Indianapolis 500.

pairs.question: What incident during the race caused Dan Wheldon to drop from the lead to third place?
pairs.answer: During Wheldon's final pit stop on lap 185, one of his crewmen dropped a lug nut, causing him to rejoin the track in third place.

pairs.question: How did Sam Hornish Jr.'s engine stall during the race affect the final outcome?
pairs.answer: Hornish Jr. stalled his engine during his last pit stop on lap 193, which allowed Castroneves to take the lead and ultimately win the race.

pairs.question: What was the average speed of the race winner, Hélio Castroneves, and how did this compare to other IndyCar races?
pairs.answer: Castroneves accumulated an average speed of 185.710 mph (298.871 km/h), making this the third-fastest race in IndyCar history at the time.

pairs.question: What significance did the 2006 Bombardier Learjet 500 have in terms of IndyCar chassis standardization?
pairs.answer: The 2006 Bombardier Learjet 500 was the first-ever IndyCar race where all entrants used the single Dallara chassis.
